revision.text:
'''Tamagushihime''' and '''Tamayorihime''' appear in [[Japanese mythology]]. Woman|Woman]]. She is known as the mother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], the first empress of Japan, [[Kushimikata-no-mikata]], a distant ancestor of the Miwa clan, and [[Kamigamo Shrine]], the deity of [[Kamo Bessarai Shrine]]. She is also known as '''Princess Mishima-Mizo''', '''Seiyadatarahihime''', '''Katsutamayori-biyorihime''' and '''Kimikahihime'''.
He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]])<ref>''[[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]''. </ref>. "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface'''' is Tamagushi Ehime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].
=== Jimmu-Ansei-tennouki, Jimmu-tennouki, Tennou-honki === According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a person (name unknown) was sent to the Emperor.
In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
=== 崇神天皇紀・崇神天皇記 === 『日本書紀』[[Emperor Sujin|崇神天皇]]段によれば、茅渟県陶邑にいた大田田根子に天皇が「汝は其れ誰が子ぞ。」と問うたところ、大田田根子は
「父をば[[Ōmononushi|大物主大神]]と曰。母をば'''活玉依媛'''と曰。陶津耳の女なり（亦は云う、奇日方天日方、武茅渟祇の女なり）。」
と言ったという。
『古事記』崇神天皇段によれば、[[Kawachi Province|河内]]の美努村にいた意富多多泥古に天皇が「汝は誰が子ぞ。」と問うたところ、意富多多泥古は
「僕は[[Ōmononushi|大物主大神]]、陶津耳命の女'''活玉依毘売'''を娶りて生みませる子、名は櫛御方命の子、飯肩巣見命の子、建甕槌命の子、僕意富多多泥古」
と言ったという。
また、『古事記』には次のように書かれている。
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. 爾（かれ）即ち鉤穴より出でし状を知りて、糸の從（まにま）に尋ね行きしかば、[[Mount Miwa|美和山]]に至りて、[[Ōmiwa Shrine|神の社]]に留りき。故其の神の子なりとは知りぬ。故其の麻の三勾（みわ）遺れるに因りて、其の地を名づけて美和と謂うなり。〈此の意富多多泥古の命は、神君、[[Kamo clan|鴨君]]の祖なり。〉」
=== 神社伝承 === [[Kifune Shrine|貴船神社]]の創始伝承には玉依姫命が、黄色い船に乗って[[Yodo River|淀川]]・[[Kamo River|鴨川]]・貴船川を遡って当地に上陸し、[[Suijin|水神]]を祭ったのに始まるとされている。
== 諸説 == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

revision.comment: /* 崇神天皇紀・崇神天皇記 */
revision.text:
'''Tamagushihime''' and '''Tamayorihime''' appear in [[Japanese mythology]]. Woman|Woman]]. She is known as the mother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], the first empress of Japan, [[Kushimikata-no-mikata]], a distant ancestor of the Miwa clan, and [[Kamigamo Shrine]], the deity of [[Kamo Bessarai Shrine]]. She is also known as '''Princess Mishima-Mizo''', '''Seiyadatarahihime''', '''Katsutamayori-biyorihime''' and '''Kimikahihime'''.
He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]])<ref>''[[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]''. </ref>. "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface'''' is Tamagushi Ehime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].
=== Jimmu-Ansei-tennouki, Jimmu-tennouki, Tennou-honki === According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a person (name unknown) was sent to the Emperor.
In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
=== Emperor Sujin Chronicle and Emperor Sujin Chronicle === According to the [[Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] section of the Chronicles of Japan, the emperor asked Otadaneko, who was at Toh-eup in the province of black porgy, "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"''My father is [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]],'' he said. The mother is called Katsutamayorihime. She is the woman of Tōtsumimi.
And she is said to have said
According to the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), the emperor asked Yitutadaro, who was in the village of Mitumu in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"I am the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iyashikasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
The name is the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iikagasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
In the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), it is written as follows.
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== 神社伝承 === [[Kifune Shrine|貴船神社]]の創始伝承には玉依姫命が、黄色い船に乗って[[Yodo River|淀川]]・[[Kamo River|鴨川]]・貴船川を遡って当地に上陸し、[[Suijin|水神]]を祭ったのに始まるとされている。
== 諸説 == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

== 参考文献 ==

"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. === Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].

In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
=== Emperor Sujin Chronicle and Emperor Sujin Chronicle === According to the [[Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] section of the Chronicles of Japan, the emperor asked Otadaneko, who was at Toh-eup in the province of black porgy, "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"''My father is [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]],'' he said. The mother is called Katsutamayorihime. She is the woman of Tōtsumimi.
And she is said to have said
According to the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), the emperor asked Yitutadaro, who was in the village of Mitumu in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"I am the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iyashikasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
The name is the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iikagasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
In the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), it is written as follows.
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].
== 諸説 == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.

[[Category:History of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

'''Tamagushihime''' and '''Tamayorihime''' appear in [[Japanese mythology]]. Woman|Woman]]. She is known as the mother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], the first empress of Japan, [[Kushimikata-no-mikata]], a distant ancestor of the Miwa clan, and [[Kamigamo Shrine]], the deity of [[Kamo Bessarai Shrine]]. She is also known as '''Princess Mishima-Mizo''', '''Seiyadatarahihime''', '''Katsutamayori-biyorihime''' and '''Kimikahihime'''.
He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]])<ref>''[[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]''. </ref>. "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface'''' is Tamagushi Ehime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].

He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]])<ref>''[[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]''. </ref>. "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface'''' is Tamagushi Ehime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].
=== Jimmu-Ansei-tennouki, Jimmu-tennouki, Tennou-honki === According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a person (name unknown) was sent to the Emperor.
In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
=== Emperor Sujin Chronicle and Emperor Sujin Chronicle === According to the [[Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] section of the Chronicles of Japan, the emperor asked Otadaneko, who was at Toh-eup in the province of black porgy, "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"''My father is [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]],'' he said. The mother is called Katsutamayorihime. She is the woman of Tōtsumimi.
And she is said to have said
According to the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), the emperor asked Yitutadaro, who was in the village of Mitumu in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"I am the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iyashikasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
The name is the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iikagasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
In the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), it is written as follows.
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].
== Theories == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.

I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].
== Theories == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.

{{merge|Tamayori-hime|discuss=Talk:Tamayori-hime#Merger proposal|date=October 2021}}
'''Tamagushihime''' and '''Tamayorihime''' appear in [[Japanese mythology]]. She is known as the mother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], the first empress of Japan, [[Kushimikata-no-mikata]], a distant ancestor of the Miwa clan, and [[Kamigamo Shrine]], the deity of [[Kamo Bessarai Shrine]]. She is also known as '''Princess Mishima-Mizo''', '''Seiyadatarahihime''', '''Katsutamayori-biyorihime''' and '''Kimikahihime'''.
He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]) ([[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]) "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface'''' is Tamagushi Ehime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].
=== Jimmu-Ansei-tennouki, Jimmu-tennouki, Tennou-honki === According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a person (name unknown) was sent to the Emperor.
In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
=== Emperor Sujin Chronicle and Emperor Sujin Chronicle === According to the [[Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] section of the Chronicles of Japan, the emperor asked Otadaneko, who was at Toh-eup in the province of black porgy, "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"''My father is [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]],'' he said. The mother is called Katsutamayorihime. She is the woman of Tōtsumimi.
And she is said to have said
According to the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), the emperor asked Yitutadaro, who was in the village of Mitumu in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"I am the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iyashikasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
The name is the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iikagasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
In the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), it is written as follows.
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].
== Theories == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 玉櫛媛 | 神祇 = | 全名 = 玉櫛媛 | 別名 = 玉櫛姫、玉依媛、玉依姫、三島溝樴姫、勢夜陀多良比売、活玉依毘売、奇美加比咩 | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = [[三島溝橛耳神]]（陶津耳命） | 母 = [[伊賀古夜日売]] | 兄弟姉妹 = [[玉依彦命]] | 配偶者 = [[事代主神]]
[[大物主大神]]（事代主と同神説あり[[宝賀寿男]]「大己貴神とその神統譜」『古代氏族の研究⑦　三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年、52、53頁。[[村島秀次]]「「大物主神 = 事代主神」論」『歴史研究』618号、2014年。）
[[火雷神]] | 子 = 事代主神との子：[[天日方奇日方命]]、[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]、[[五十鈴依媛命]]
大物主神との子：[[櫛御方命]]、[[比売多多良伊須気余理比売]]
火雷神との子：[[賀茂別雷命]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[賀茂御祖神社]] など | 記紀 = | 関連氏族 = [[賀茂氏]]、[[三輪氏]] }} '''玉櫛媛'''（たまくしひめ、'''玉櫛姫'''）、'''玉依媛'''（たまよりひめ、'''玉依姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[女性]]。初代[[皇后]]の[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]や、[[三輪氏]]遠祖の[[櫛御方命]]、[[賀茂別雷神社]]祭神の[[賀茂別雷命]]などの母として知られる。'''三島溝樴姫'''、'''勢夜陀多良比売'''、'''活玉依毘売'''、'''奇美加比咩'''ともいう。 [[鴨建角身命]]（[[八咫烏]]）の子であり、兄に[[鴨建玉依彦命]]（[[賀茂氏#天神系|賀茂氏]]の祖）がいる『[[#keizu|古代豪族系図集覧]]』。。「玉依媛」・「玉依彦」のように、[[ヒメ]]と[[ヒコ]]の二者（この場合は兄妹）がペアで統治を行う体制は[[ヒメヒコ制]]と呼ばれる。 == 記録 == 以下、'''太字'''にした人名は玉櫛媛のことである。 === 山城国風土記 === 『[[山城国風土記]]』によれば、[[賀茂建角身命]]は[[丹波国]][[神野]]の神[[伊賀古夜日売]]をめとり、[[玉依日子]]（[[賀茂県主]]の遠祖）と'''玉依日売'''の二子を生んだ。玉依日売が[[鴨川 (淀川水系)|石河瀬見小川]]の辺に遊んでいた時、丹塗矢（[[火雷神]]の化身）が川上より流れ下ってきた。これを取り床の辺にさし置くと、玉依日売は妊娠して子を生んだ。この子は賀茂建角身命の名からとって[[賀茂別雷命]]と名付けたという。 賀茂建角身命および玉依日売は[[賀茂御祖神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社]]の祭神である。この二社は[[賀茂神社]]と総称される。これに関連して、各地に[[加茂神社|賀茂神社・加茂神社]]が存在する。 === 神代紀・地祇本紀 === 『[[日本書紀]]』第8段の第6の一書では、「又曰」として、[[事代主神]]が八尋熊[[和邇|鰐]]となって'''三島溝樴姫'''（みしまのみぞくいひめ。或いは'''玉櫛姫'''という。）に通って生まれた子が[[姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]]（[[神武天皇]]の[[皇后]]）であるとする。 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[地祇本紀]]」によれば、[[都味歯八重事代主神]]（つみはやえことしろぬし の かみ）は八尋熊鰐となって[[三島湟咋|三島溝杭]]（みしまのみぞくい）の娘'''活玉依姫'''（いくたまより-）に通い、以下の三子を生んだという。 * [[天日方奇日方命]]（あめのひがたくしひがた の みこと、阿田都久志尼命） *: [[神武天皇]]の時代に[[申食国政大夫]]となる。[[三輪氏]]・[[賀茂氏#地祇系|賀茂氏]]の祖。 * [[姫踏韛五十鈴姫命]]（ひめたたらいすずひめ の みこと） *: [[神武天皇]]の[[皇后]]で、[[綏靖天皇]]などの母。 * [[五十鈴依姫命]]（いすずよりひめ の みこと） *: [[綏靖天皇]]の皇后で、[[安寧天皇]]の母。 === 神武-安寧天皇紀・神武天皇記・天皇本紀 === 『日本書紀』[[神武天皇]]段によれば、人（名は不明）が天皇に 「[[事代主神]][[三嶋湟咋|三島溝橛耳神]]の女'''玉櫛媛'''に共して生める児、号を[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]と曰う。是国色の秀者。」 と奏したという。[[綏靖天皇]]段によれば、媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命は事代主神の娘だという。[[安寧天皇]]段によれば、[[五十鈴依媛命]]も事代主神の娘であるという。 『先代旧事本紀』の「[[天皇本紀]]」によれば、人（名は不明）が神武天皇に 「事代主神三島溝橛耳神の女'''玉櫛媛'''と生める児、号を媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命と曰う。是国色の秀者なり。」 と奏したという。また、媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命および[[五十鈴依媛命]]は事代主神の娘であるという。 『[[古事記]]』[[神武天皇]]段によれば、神武天皇が[[大后]]とする美人を求めた時、[[大久米命]]が 「此間（ここ）に媛女（おとめ）有り、是神御子と謂う。其を神御子なりと謂（もう）す所以（ゆえ）は、[[三嶋湟咋]]の女、名は'''勢夜陀多良比売'''、其れ容姿麗美故、[[大神神社|美和]]の[[大物主神]]、見感でて、其の美人（おとめ）の大便為すの時に、丹塗矢と化（な）りて、其の大便為すの溝の流下より、其の美人の[[女性器|ほと]]を突きたまいき。爾（かれ）其の美人驚きて、立ち走りいすすきき。乃（か）くて其の矢を将来して、床辺に置きしかば、忽ちに麗しき壮夫（おとこ）に成りて、即ち其の美人を娶りて子を生む。名は[[富登多多良伊須須岐比売命]]と謂（もう）す。亦の名は[[比売多多良伊須気余理比売]]と謂す。故（かれ）是（ここ）を以て神御子とは謂すなり。」 といったという。 === 崇神天皇紀・崇神天皇記 === 『日本書紀』[[崇神天皇]]段によれば、[[茅渟県]][[陶邑]]にいた[[大田田根子]]に天皇が「汝は其れ誰が子ぞ。」と問うたところ、大田田根子は 「父をば[[大物主大神]]と曰。母をば'''活玉依媛'''と曰。陶津耳の女なり（亦は云う、奇日方天日方、武茅渟祇の女なり）。」 と言ったという。 『古事記』崇神天皇段によれば、[[河内国|河内]]の[[美努村]]にいた[[意富多多泥古]]に天皇が「汝は誰が子ぞ。」と問うたところ、意富多多泥古は 「僕は[[大物主大神]]、[[陶津耳命]]の女'''活玉依毘売'''を娶りて生みませる子、名は[[櫛御方命]]の子、[[飯肩巣見命]]の子、[[建甕槌命]]の子、僕意富多多泥古」 と言ったという。 また、『古事記』には次のように書かれている。 「此の意富多多泥古人と謂うを、神の子と知れる所以は、上に云える活玉依毘売、其れ容姿端正。是に神壮夫有りて、其の形姿威儀、時に比無し。夜半の時、倏忽と到来す。故相感でて、共婚供住の間、未だ幾時を経ず、其の美人妊身す。爾（ここ）に父母其の妊身の事を怪しみて、其の女に問いて曰く、『汝は自ずから妊めり。夫無きに、何の由にか妊身（はら）みぬ。』ととえば、答えて曰く、『麗美（うるわ）しき壮夫有り。其の姓名を知らず。夕毎に到来し、供住の間に、自然（おのず）から懷妊（はら）みぬ。』という。是を以て其の父母其の人を知らんと欲して、其の女に誨えて曰く、『赤土を以て床の前に散らし、へその紡麻を以て針に貫きて、其の衣の襴（すそ）に刺せ。』とおしう。故教の如くして旦時に見れば、針に著けたる麻は、戸の鉤穴より控き通りて出で、唯遺れる麻は三勾のみなりき。爾（かれ）即ち鉤穴より出でし状を知りて、糸の從（まにま）に尋ね行きしかば、[[美和山]]に至りて、[[大神神社|神の社]]に留りき。故其の神の子なりとは知りぬ。故其の麻の三勾（みわ）遺れるに因りて、其の地を名づけて美和と謂うなり。〈此の意富多多泥古の命は、[[三輪氏|神君]]、[[賀茂氏#地祇系|鴨君]]の祖なり。〉」 === 神社伝承 === [[貴船神社]]の創始伝承には玉依姫命が、黄色い船に乗って[[淀川]]・[[鴨川_(淀川水系)|鴨川]]・貴船川を遡って当地に上陸し、[[水神]]を祭ったのに始まるとされている。 == 諸説 == 『古事記』に記述されている大物主との神婚譚に関しては、『土佐国風土記』逸文に類した話があり、三輪山神婚説話に分類される（後述書 p.136）。この三輪山神婚説話の類型は、ユーラシアの北半球から北アメリカ、そして朝鮮半島からモンゴルにまで分布する、太陽（妹）に月（兄）が恋をし、夜に来訪する恋人の正体をつかもうとして、太陽は恋人の顔に煤（すす）をつけ、朝になって兄の顔に煤がついていた、という話のモチーフが変化したものが三輪山神婚説話であるという考え（坂本太郎 他注『日本書紀 上』）もある（志田諄一 『<日本史=21> 風土記の世界』 教育者 1979年 p.137）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[近藤敏喬]]|title=古代豪族系図集覧|year=1993|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=156頁|chapter=|ref=keizu}} == 関連項目 == * [[タマヨリビメ]] * [[溝咋神社]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{merge|Tamayori-hime|discuss=Talk:Tamayori-hime#Merger proposal|date=October 2021}}
'''Tamagushihime''' and '''Tamayorihime''' appear in [[Japanese mythology]]. She is known as the mother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], the first empress of Japan, [[Kushimikata-no-mikata]], a distant ancestor of the Miwa clan, and [[Kamigamo Shrine]], the deity of [[Kamo Bessarai Shrine]]. She is also known as '''Princess Mishima-Mizo''', '''Seiyadatarahihime''', '''Katsutamayori-biyorihime''' and '''Kimikahihime'''.
He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]) ([[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]) "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface''' is Tamagushihime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].
=== Jimmu-Ansei-tennouki, Jimmu-tennouki, Tennou-honki === According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a person (name unknown) was sent to the Emperor.
In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
=== Emperor Sujin Chronicle and Emperor Sujin Chronicle === According to the [[Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] section of the Chronicles of Japan, the emperor asked Otadaneko, who was at Toh-eup in the province of black porgy, "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"''My father is [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]],'' he said. The mother is called Katsutamayorihime. She is the woman of Tōtsumimi.
And she is said to have said
According to the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), the emperor asked Yitutadaro, who was in the village of Mitumu in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"I am the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iyashikasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
The name is the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iikagasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
In the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), it is written as follows.
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].
== Theories == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.
== Related topics == * [[List of Japanese deities]]
== References == {{Reflist}} * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Genealogical Catalogue of the Australian Families|publisher=Tokyo Do Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|author=Toshitaka Kondo|page=156|chapter=|ref= keizu|language=ja}} * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Family Tree Collection|publisher=Tokyo Dome Publishing|year=1993|isbn= 4-490-20225-3|author=Toshitaka Kondo|pages=156|chapter=|ref=keizu|language=ja}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たまくしひめ}}

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 玉櫛媛 | 神祇 = | 全名 = 玉櫛媛 | 別名 = 玉櫛姫、玉依媛、玉依姫、三島溝樴姫、勢夜陀多良比売、活玉依毘売、奇美加比咩、賀屋鳴比女命 | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = [[三島溝橛耳神]]（陶津耳命） | 母 = [[伊賀古夜日売]] | 兄弟姉妹 = [[玉依彦命]]
[[加理波夜須多祁比波預命]]（一説に長子） | 配偶者 = [[事代主神]]
[[大物主大神]]（事代主と同神説あり[[宝賀寿男]]「大己貴神とその神統譜」『古代氏族の研究⑦　三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年、52、53頁。[[村島秀次]]「「大物主神 = 事代主神」論」『歴史研究』618号、2014年。）
[[火雷神]]
[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]][[中田憲信]]『神別系譜』、[[東京国立博物館]]デジタルライブラリー。 | 子 = 事代主神との子：[[天日方奇日方命]]、[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]、[[五十鈴依媛命]]
大物主神との子：[[櫛御方命]]、[[比売多多良伊須気余理比売]]
火雷神との子：[[賀茂別雷命]]
賀夜奈流美命との子：[[鴨主命]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[賀茂御祖神社]] など | 記紀 = | 関連氏族 = [[賀茂県主]]、[[大神氏|三輪君]] }} '''玉櫛媛'''（たまくしひめ、'''玉櫛姫'''）、'''玉依媛'''（たまよりひめ、'''玉依姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[女性]]。初代[[皇后]]の[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]や、[[三輪氏]]遠祖の[[櫛御方命]]、[[賀茂別雷神社]]祭神の[[賀茂別雷命]]などの母として知られる。'''三島溝樴姫'''（みしまのみぞくいひめ）、'''勢夜陀多良比売'''（せやだたらひめ）、'''活玉依毘売'''（いくたまよりびめ）、'''奇美加比咩'''（くしみかひめ）、'''賀屋鳴比女命'''（かやなるひめのみこと）ともいう。 [[鴨建角身命]]（[[八咫烏]]）の子であり、兄に[[鴨建玉依彦命]]（[[賀茂氏#天神系|賀茂氏]]の祖）がいる『[[#keizu|古代豪族系図集覧]]』。。「玉依媛」・「玉依彦」のように、[[ヒメ]]と[[ヒコ]]の二者（この場合は兄妹）がペアで統治を行う体制は[[ヒメヒコ制]]と呼ばれる。 == 記録 == 以下、'''太字'''にした人名は玉櫛媛のことである。 === 山城国風土記 === 『[[山城国風土記]]』によれば、[[賀茂建角身命]]は[[丹波国]][[神野]]の神[[伊賀古夜日売]]をめとり、[[玉依日子]]（[[賀茂県主]]の遠祖）と'''玉依日売'''の二子を生んだ。玉依日売が[[鴨川 (淀川水系)|石河瀬見小川]]の辺に遊んでいた時、丹塗矢（[[火雷神]]の化身）が川上より流れ下ってきた。これを取り床の辺にさし置くと、玉依日売は妊娠して子を生んだ。この子は賀茂建角身命の名からとって[[賀茂別雷命]]と名付けたという。 賀茂建角身命および玉依日売は[[賀茂御祖神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社]]の祭神である。この二社は[[賀茂神社]]と総称される。これに関連して、各地に[[加茂神社|賀茂神社・加茂神社]]が存在する。 === 神代紀・地祇本紀 === 『[[日本書紀]]』第8段の第6の一書では、「又曰」として、[[事代主神]]が八尋熊[[和邇|鰐]]となって'''三島溝樴姫'''（みしまのみぞくいひめ。或いは'''玉櫛姫'''という。）に通って生まれた子が[[姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]]（[[神武天皇]]の[[皇后]]）であるとする。 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[地祇本紀]]」によれば、[[都味歯八重事代主神]]（つみはやえことしろぬし の かみ）は八尋熊鰐となって[[三島湟咋|三島溝杭]]（みしまのみぞくい）の娘'''活玉依姫'''（いくたまより-）に通い、以下の三子を生んだという。 * [[天日方奇日方命]]（あめのひがたくしひがた の みこと、阿田都久志尼命） *: [[神武天皇]]の時代に[[申食国政大夫]]となる。[[三輪氏]]・[[賀茂氏#地祇系|賀茂氏]]の祖。 * [[姫踏韛五十鈴姫命]]（ひめたたらいすずひめ の みこと） *: [[神武天皇]]の[[皇后]]で、[[綏靖天皇]]などの母。 * [[五十鈴依姫命]]（いすずよりひめ の みこと） *: [[綏靖天皇]]の皇后で、[[安寧天皇]]の母。 === 神武-安寧天皇紀・神武天皇記・天皇本紀 === 『日本書紀』[[神武天皇]]段によれば、人（名は不明）が天皇に 「[[事代主神]][[三嶋湟咋|三島溝橛耳神]]の女'''玉櫛媛'''に共して生める児、号を[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]と曰う。是国色の秀者。」 と奏したという。[[綏靖天皇]]段によれば、媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命は事代主神の娘だという。[[安寧天皇]]段によれば、[[五十鈴依媛命]]も事代主神の娘であるという。 『先代旧事本紀』の「[[天皇本紀]]」によれば、人（名は不明）が神武天皇に 「事代主神三島溝橛耳神の女'''玉櫛媛'''と生める児、号を媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命と曰う。是国色の秀者なり。」 と奏したという。また、媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命および[[五十鈴依媛命]]は事代主神の娘であるという。 『[[古事記]]』[[神武天皇]]段によれば、神武天皇が[[大后]]とする美人を求めた時、[[大久米命]]が 「此間（ここ）に媛女（おとめ）有り、是神御子と謂う。其を神御子なりと謂（もう）す所以（ゆえ）は、[[三嶋湟咋]]の女、名は'''勢夜陀多良比売'''、其れ容姿麗美故、[[大神神社|美和]]の[[大物主神]]、見感でて、其の美人（おとめ）の大便為すの時に、丹塗矢と化（な）りて、其の大便為すの溝の流下より、其の美人の[[女性器|ほと]]を突きたまいき。爾（かれ）其の美人驚きて、立ち走りいすすきき。乃（か）くて其の矢を将来して、床辺に置きしかば、忽ちに麗しき壮夫（おとこ）に成りて、即ち其の美人を娶りて子を生む。名は[[富登多多良伊須須岐比売命]]と謂（もう）す。亦の名は[[比売多多良伊須気余理比売]]と謂す。故（かれ）是（ここ）を以て神御子とは謂すなり。」 といったという。 === 崇神天皇紀・崇神天皇記 === 『日本書紀』[[崇神天皇]]段によれば、[[茅渟県]][[陶邑]]にいた[[大田田根子]]に天皇が「汝は其れ誰が子ぞ。」と問うたところ、大田田根子は 「父をば[[大物主大神]]と曰。母をば'''活玉依媛'''と曰。陶津耳の女なり（亦は云う、奇日方天日方、武茅渟祇の女なり）。」 と言ったという。 『古事記』崇神天皇段によれば、[[河内国|河内]]の[[美努村]]にいた[[意富多多泥古]]に天皇が「汝は誰が子ぞ。」と問うたところ、意富多多泥古は 「僕は[[大物主大神]]、[[陶津耳命]]の女'''活玉依毘売'''を娶りて生みませる子、名は[[櫛御方命]]の子、[[飯肩巣見命]]の子、[[建甕槌命]]の子、僕意富多多泥古」 と言ったという。 また、『古事記』には次のように書かれている。 「此の意富多多泥古人と謂うを、神の子と知れる所以は、上に云える活玉依毘売、其れ容姿端正。是に神壮夫有りて、其の形姿威儀、時に比無し。夜半の時、倏忽と到来す。故相感でて、共婚供住の間、未だ幾時を経ず、其の美人妊身す。爾（ここ）に父母其の妊身の事を怪しみて、其の女に問いて曰く、『汝は自ずから妊めり。夫無きに、何の由にか妊身（はら）みぬ。』ととえば、答えて曰く、『麗美（うるわ）しき壮夫有り。其の姓名を知らず。夕毎に到来し、供住の間に、自然（おのず）から懷妊（はら）みぬ。』という。是を以て其の父母其の人を知らんと欲して、其の女に誨えて曰く、『赤土を以て床の前に散らし、へその紡麻を以て針に貫きて、其の衣の襴（すそ）に刺せ。』とおしう。故教の如くして旦時に見れば、針に著けたる麻は、戸の鉤穴より控き通りて出で、唯遺れる麻は三勾のみなりき。爾（かれ）即ち鉤穴より出でし状を知りて、糸の從（まにま）に尋ね行きしかば、[[美和山]]に至りて、[[大神神社|神の社]]に留りき。故其の神の子なりとは知りぬ。故其の麻の三勾（みわ）遺れるに因りて、其の地を名づけて美和と謂うなり。〈此の意富多多泥古の命は、[[三輪氏|神君]]、[[賀茂氏#地祇系|鴨君]]の祖なり。〉」 === 神社伝承 === [[貴船神社]]の創始伝承には玉依姫命が、黄色い船に乗って[[淀川]]・[[鴨川_(淀川水系)|鴨川]]・貴船川を遡って当地に上陸し、[[水神]]を祭ったのに始まるとされている。 == 諸説 == 『古事記』に記述されている大物主との神婚譚に関しては、『土佐国風土記』逸文に類した話があり、三輪山神婚説話に分類される（後述書 p.136）。この三輪山神婚説話の類型は、ユーラシアの北半球から北アメリカ、そして朝鮮半島からモンゴルにまで分布する、太陽（妹）に月（兄）が恋をし、夜に来訪する恋人の正体をつかもうとして、太陽は恋人の顔に煤（すす）をつけ、朝になって兄の顔に煤がついていた、という話のモチーフが変化したものが三輪山神婚説話であるという考え（坂本太郎 他注『日本書紀 上』）もある（志田諄一 『<日本史=21> 風土記の世界』 教育者 1979年 p.137）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[近藤敏喬]]|title=古代豪族系図集覧|year=1993|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=156頁|chapter=|ref=keizu}} == 関連項目 == * [[タマヨリビメ]] * [[溝咋神社]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] {{神道 横}} [[大物主大神]]（事代主と同神説あり[[宝賀寿男]]「大己貴神とその神統譜」『古代氏族の研究⑦　三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年、52、53頁。[[村島秀次]]「「大物主神 = 事代主神」論」『歴史研究』618号、2014年。）
[[火雷神]]
[[賀夜奈流美命]][[中田憲信]]『神別系譜』、[[東京国立博物館]]デジタルライブラリー。 | 子 = 事代主神との子：[[天日方奇日方命]]、[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]、[[五十鈴依媛命]]
大物主神との子：[[櫛御方命]]、[[比売多多良伊須気余理比売]]
火雷神との子：[[賀茂別雷命]]
賀夜奈流美命との子：[[鴨主命]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[賀茂御祖神社]] など | 記紀 = | 関連氏族 = [[賀茂県主]]、[[大神氏|三輪君]] }} '''玉櫛媛'''（たまくしひめ、'''玉櫛姫'''）、'''玉依媛'''（たまよりひめ、'''玉依姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[女性]]。初代[[皇后]]の[[媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]や、[[三輪氏]]遠祖の[[櫛御方命]]、[[賀茂別雷神社]]祭神の[[賀茂別雷命]]などの母として知られる。'''三島溝樴姫'''（みしまのみぞくいひめ）、'''勢夜陀多良比売'''（せやだたらひめ）、'''活玉依毘売'''（いくたまよりびめ）、'''奇美加比咩'''（くしみかひめ）、'''賀屋鳴比女命'''（かやなるひめのみこと）ともいう。 [[鴨建角身命]]（[[八咫烏]]）の子であり、兄に[[鴨建玉依彦命]]（[[賀茂氏#天神系|賀茂氏]]の祖）がいる『[[#keizu|古代豪族系図集覧]]』。。「玉依媛」・「玉依彦」のように、[[ヒメ]]と[[ヒコ]]の二者（この場合は兄妹）がペアで統治を行う体制は[[ヒメヒコ制]]と呼ばれる。 == 記録 == 以下、'''太字'''にした人名は玉櫛媛のことである。 === 山城国風土記 === 『[[山城国風土記]]』によれば、[[賀茂建角身命]]は[[丹波国]][[神野]]の神[[伊賀古夜日売]]をめとり、[[玉依日子]]（[[賀茂県主]]の遠祖）と'''玉依日売'''の二子を生んだ。玉依日売が[[鴨川 (淀川水系)|石河瀬見小川]]の辺に遊んでいた時、丹塗矢（[[火雷神]]の化身）が川上より流れ下ってきた。これを取り床の辺にさし置くと、玉依日売は妊娠して子を生んだ。この子は賀茂建角身命の名からとって[[賀茂別雷命]]と名付けたという。 賀茂建角身命および玉依日売は[[賀茂御祖神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社]]の祭神である。この二社は[[賀茂神社]]と総称される。これに関連して、各地に[[加茂神社|賀茂神社・加茂神社]]が存在する。 === 神代紀・地祇本紀 === 『[[日本書紀]]』第8段の第6の一書では、「又曰」として、[[事代主神]]が八尋熊[[和邇|鰐]]となって'''三島溝樴姫'''（みしまのみぞくいひめ。或いは'''玉櫛姫'''という。）に通って生まれた子が[[姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]]（[[神武天皇]]の[[皇后]]）であるとする。 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[地祇本紀]]」によれば、[[都味歯八重事代主神]]（つみはやえことしろぬし の かみ）は八尋熊鰐となって[[三島湟咋|三島溝杭]]（みしまのみぞくい）の娘'''活玉依姫'''（いくたまより-）に通い、以下の三子を生んだという。 * [[天日方奇日方命]]（あめのひがたくしひがた の みこと、阿田都久志尼命） *: [[神武天皇]]の時代に[[申食国政大夫]]となる。[[三輪氏]]・[[賀茂氏#地祇系|賀茂氏]]の祖。 * [[姫踏韛五十鈴姫命]]（ひめたたらいすずひめ の みこと） *: [[神武天皇]]の[[皇后]]で、[[綏靖天皇]]などの母。 * [[五十鈴依姫命]]（いすずよりひめ の みこと） *: [[綏靖天皇]]の皇后で、[[安寧天皇]]の母。 === 神武-安寧天皇紀・神武天皇記・天皇本紀 === 『日本書紀』[[神武天皇]]段によれば、人（名は不明）が天皇に 「此間（ここ）に媛女（おとめ）有り、是神御子と謂う。其を神御子なりと謂（もう）す所以（ゆえ）は、[[三嶋湟咋]]の女、名は'''勢夜陀多良比売'''、其れ容姿麗美故、[[大神神社|美和]]の[[大物主神]]、見感でて、其の美人（おとめ）の大便為すの時に、丹塗矢と化（な）りて、其の大便為すの溝の流下より、其の美人の[[女性器|ほと]]を突きたまいき。爾（かれ）其の美人驚きて、立ち走りいすすきき。乃（か）くて其の矢を将来して、床辺に置きしかば、忽ちに麗しき壮夫（おとこ）に成りて、即ち其の美人を娶りて子を生む。名は[[富登多多良伊須須岐比売命]]と謂（もう）す。亦の名は[[比売多多良伊須気余理比売]]と謂す。故（かれ）是（ここ）を以て神御子とは謂すなり。」 といったという。 === 崇神天皇紀・崇神天皇記 === 『日本書紀』[[崇神天皇]]段によれば、[[茅渟県]][[陶邑]]にいた[[大田田根子]]に天皇が「汝は其れ誰が子ぞ。」と問うたところ、大田田根子は 「父をば[[大物主大神]]と曰。母をば'''活玉依媛'''と曰。陶津耳の女なり（亦は云う、奇日方天日方、武茅渟祇の女なり）。」 と言ったという。 {{more footnotes|date=January 2022}}
{{merge|Tamayori-hime|discuss=Talk:Tamayori-hime#Merger proposal|date=October 2021}}{{Expand language |langcode=ja |otherarticle={{{1|}}} |section={{{section|}}} |date=October 2020|fa={{{fa|}}} |topic= |nodoc={{{nodoc|}}} |no interwiki={{{no interwiki|}}} }}
'''Tamagushihime''' and '''Tamayorihime''' appear in [[Japanese mythology]]. She is known as the mother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], the first empress of Japan, [[Kushimikata-no-mikata]], a distant ancestor of the Miwa clan, and [[Kamigamo Shrine]], the deity of [[Kamo Bessarai Shrine]]. She is also known as '''Princess Mishima-Mizo''', '''Seiyadatarahihime''', '''Katsutamayori-biyorihime''' and '''Kimikahihime'''.
He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]) ([[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]) "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface''' is Tamagushihime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].
=== Jimmu-Ansei-tennouki, Jimmu-tennouki, Tennou-honki === According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a person (name unknown) was sent to the Emperor.
In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
=== Emperor Sujin Chronicle and Emperor Sujin Chronicle === According to the [[Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] section of the Chronicles of Japan, the emperor asked Otadaneko, who was at Toh-eup in the province of black porgy, "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"''My father is [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]],'' he said. The mother is called Katsutamayorihime. She is the woman of Tōtsumimi.
And she is said to have said
According to the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), the emperor asked Yitutadaro, who was in the village of Mitumu in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"I am the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iyashikasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
The name is the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iikagasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
In the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), it is written as follows.
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].
== Theories == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.
== Related topics == * [[List of Japanese deities]]
== References == {{Reflist}} * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Genealogical Catalogue of the Australian Families|publisher=Tokyo Do Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|author=Toshitaka Kondo|page=156|chapter=|ref= keizu|language=ja}} * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Family Tree Collection|publisher=Tokyo Dome Publishing|year=1993|isbn= 4-490-20225-3|author=Toshitaka Kondo|pages=156|chapter=|ref=keizu|language=ja}}

=== Emperor Sujin Chronicle and Emperor Sujin Chronicle === According to the [[Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] section of the Chronicles of Japan, the emperor asked Otadaneko, who was at Toh-eup in the province of black porgy, "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"''My father is [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]],'' he said. The mother is called Katsutamayorihime. She is the woman of Tōtsumimi.
And she is said to have said
According to the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), the emperor asked Yitutadaro, who was in the village of Mitumu in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]], "Whose son art thou? Who is your son?
"I am the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iyashikasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
The name is the son of Kushimikata-no-mikoto, the son of Iikagasumi-no-mikoto, the son of Tatemikazuchi-no-mikoto.
In the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters), it is written as follows.
"The reason why this man is known as the son of a god is that he is a goddess of beauty. The reason why he is known as the son of the gods is because he is the one who is said to be the goddess of life. In the middle of the night, he suddenly appears. The two of them feel each other's presence, and they marry and live together for many years, until they become pregnant with a beautiful woman. Then her father and mother, suspicious of her fertility, asked her: 'Thou art pregnant of thy own accord. How can you conceive without a husband? She replied: "I have a beautiful husband. I do not know his name. He comes every evening, and during the time he lives here, he naturally conceives. It is said. Therefore, in order to know his mother and father, he told the woman: "Scatter red earth in front of the floor, and pierce the navel with a needle, and stick it into the brocade of his garment. And pierce the brocade with a needle. When the time came, the hemp on the needle had passed through the hookhole of the door, and only three strips of hemp remained. Knowing that he was the son of a god, he came to [[Mount Miwa]] and remained there until he reached the [[Ōmiwa Shrine]]. I do not know that he is the son of that god. It is called Miwa because of the three strands of hemp that remain. This life of Yifutadaro is the ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]].
=== Shrine Lore === The origin of [[Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] is said to date back to the time when Princess Tamayori, riding a yellow boat up the [[Yodo River|Yodo River]], [[Kamo River|Kamo River]] and Kifune River, landed in this area to worship [[Suijin|Suijin]].
== Theories == The story of the divine marriage between the sun and the moon in the Kojiki is similar to that in the Tosa Kokufudoki, and is classified as the Miwasan divine marriage story (see below, p.136). The motif of the story is that the moon (elder brother) falls in love with the sun (younger sister) and tries to find out the identity of the lover who comes to visit at night. The motif of the story is that the sun dipped the lover's face in soot, and in the morning the brother's face was covered with soot.

{{more footnotes|date=January 2022}}{{Expand language |langcode=ja |otherarticle={{{1|}}} |section={{{section|}}} |date=October 2020|fa={{{fa|}}} |topic= |nodoc={{{nodoc|}}} |no interwiki={{{no interwiki|}}} }}{{Distinguish|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} '''Tamagushihime''' and '''Tamayorihime''' appear in [[Japanese mythology]]. She is known as the mother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], the first empress of Japan, [[Kushimikata-no-mikata]], a distant ancestor of the Miwa clan, and [[Kamigamo Shrine]], the deity of [[Kamo Bessarai Shrine]]. She is also known as '''Princess Mishima-Mizo''', '''Seiyadatarahihime''', '''Katsutamayori-biyorihime''' and '''Kimikahihime'''.
He is the son of Kamo Kenkakumimikoto (Yatagarasu), and has an elder brother Kamo Ken Tamayorihiko (the founder of the [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]) ([[Tamagushi Ehime#keizu|Keiretsu Shuwan]]) "A system in which two parties (in this case brother and sister) rule in pairs, as in the case of Tamayorihime and Tamayorihiko, is known as the Himehiko system.
== Record == In the following, the person's name in '''boldface''' is Tamagushihime.
=== Yamashiro Country Fudoki === According to the Yamashiro Country Fudoki, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto married Iga Koyahime, a goddess of [[Tanba Province]], and had two children, Tamayohihime (a distant ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]]) and '''Tamayohihime'''. When Tamayorihime was playing by the [[Kamo River|Ishikawasemi Stream]], a Taninuri arrow (an incarnation of the [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Thunder God]]) came down from the river. When she took it and placed it on the floor, Tamayorihime became pregnant and gave birth to a child. The child was named Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto, after Kamo Kenkakumimikoto.
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto and Tamayorihime are the deities of the [[Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Ancestral Shrine]] and Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto is the deity of the [[Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Bessaraijin Shrine]]. These two shrines are collectively known as the [[Kamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]]. In relation to this, there are Kamo and Kamo shrines in various parts of Japan.
=== [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Jishiki Honki]] === In the sixth book of the eighth section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Kotoshironushi]] is described as '''Mata ku''', meaning '''Kotoshironushi''', and '''Wani (dragon)''', meaning '''Crocodile'''. or '''Princess Tamagushi'''). (or ''Tamagushi-hime''), and that the child born to her was [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetoutaigojuzu-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]).
According to the [[Jigion-honki]] of the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo-kyoji-honki]], [[Kotoshironushi|Tsumihae Kotoshironushi-no-kami]] became an eight-legged bear crocodile and attended to the daughter of Mishima no Mizokui, '''Ikutama-yori-hime''', and gave birth to the following three children.
* [[Ame-no-higatakushi-higata-no-mikoto]] (Ata-Tokushini-no-mikoto) *: During the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]], he became the shikushoku kokusei-taibu. Founder of the Miwa clan and [[Kamo clan|Kamo clan]]. * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime no Mikoto]] * [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and mother of [[Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suiyasu]] and others. * [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyorihime no Mikoto]] *: Empress of [[Emperor Suizei|Suizei]] and mother of [[Emperor Annei|Anning]].
=== Jimmu-Ansei-tennouki, Jimmu-tennouki, Tennou-honki === According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a person (name unknown) was sent to the Emperor.
In the [[Emperor Jimmu]] stage of the [[Nihon Shoki]], a man (name unknown) tells the Emperor, "The child born to [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiroushi]], the woman '''Tamagushihime''' of Mishima-mizoosokusei-mimi, is called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Eme-Tutobi-gojusu-hime]]. This is a man of outstanding national beauty.
[[Emperor Suizuru]] is said to have said. According to the [[Emperor Suizei]], [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] was the daughter of Kotoshironushi. According to [[Emperor Annei|Emperor Anei]], [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] is also the daughter of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Kujiki|Tenno-honki]] of the [[Sachiyo-kyoji-honki]], a person (name unknown) told Emperor Jinmu
"It is said that the woman who gave birth to Tamagushihime, the daughter of the god Mishima-mizookusokidokemimi, was called Ehime-stuutoukane-Isuzuhime. Her name is Ehime Mitsuru Isuzuhime, and she is a person of great national beauty.
It is said that he said, "This is a man of great national beauty. It is said that [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]] are daughters of Kotoshironushi.
According to the [[Emperor Jimmu]] section of [[Kojiki]], when Emperor Jimmu sought a beautiful woman to be his wife, Okume-no-mikoto said
"Here is a woman, a princess, who is called the Son of God. The reason for calling her the divine Son is that she was a woman from Mishimasakoyaokui, named ''Seiyadatara-hime'', who was so beautiful in appearance that [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]] of the [[Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa]] saw her, and when she was passing urine, she turned into a tannin arrow. And when she was taking a stool, she turned into an arrow and pierced her [[Female reproductive system Then the beauty was surprised, and stood and ran. And when he had taken the arrow and placed it on the floor, he immediately became a handsome man, and married her and had children with her. His name is [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Tomitataraisuzu-hime]]. His name is also called [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. This is why he is called the Son of God.
He is said to have said.
